        "id": 28578,
        "publication_number": "US-2025031703-A1",
        "application_number": "US-202418797756-A",
        "publication_date": "2025-01-30",

        "priority_date": "2014-09-30",
        "title": "Use of pectin or pectin-related saccharides to enhance efficacy of plant growth-promoting rhizobacteria (pgpr) strains for promoting growth and health in plants and animals",
        "abstract": "To understand the growth-promoting and disease-inhibiting activities of plant growth-promoting rhizobacteria (PGPR) strains, the genomes of 12 Bacillus subtilis group strains with PGPR activity were sequenced and analyzed. These B. subtilis strains exhibited high genomic diversity, whereas the genomes of B. amyloliquefaciens strains (a member of the B. subtilis group) are highly conserved. A pairwise BLASTp matrix revealed that gene family similarity among Bacillus genomes ranges from 32-90%, with 2,839 genes within the core genome of B. amyloliquefaciens subsp. plantarum (now B. velezensis). Comparative genomic analyses of B. amyloliquefaciens strains identified genes that are linked with biological control and colonization of roots and/or leaves, including 73 genes uniquely associated with subsp. plantarum (now B. velezensis) strains that have predicted functions related to signaling, transportation, secondary metabolite production, and carbon source utilization. Although B. amyloliquefaciens (now B. velezensis) strains contain gene clusters that encode many different secondary metabolites, only polyketide biosynthetic clusters that encode difficidin and macrolactin are conserved within this subspecies. To evaluate their role in plant pathogen biocontrol, genes involved in secondary metabolite biosynthesis were deleted in B. amyloliquefaciens (now B. velezensis) strain, revealing that difficidin expression is critical in reducing the severity of disease, caused by Xanthomonas axonopodis pv. vesicatoria in tomato plants. This Example defines genomic features of PGPR strains and links them with biocontrol activity and with host colonization.",
        "assignee": "Auburn University",
        "inventor": "KLOEPPER, JOSEPH",

        "id": 5776,

        "application_number": "US-2024037618-W",

        "title": "Ãdition gÃ©nomique sans transgÃ¨ne efficace au sein de plantes dans la gÃ©nÃ©ration t0 sur la base d&#39;une stratÃ©gie de co-Ã©dition",
        "abstract": "Transgene-free genome editing of plants in T0 generation is highly desirable but challenging, especially in perennials and vegetatively propagated plants. Here is provided a co-editing strategy for generating transgene-free, gene-edited plants via Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression of cytosine base editor (CBE)/gRNA-Cas 12a/crRNA-GFP in plants. Specifically, CBE/gRNA was used to base edit the AES gene to confer resistance to herbicide chlorsulfuron as a selection marker, which has no negative effects on plant phenotypes; Casl2a/crRNA was used for editing genes(s) of interest; GFP was used for selecting transgene-free transformants. Using this approach, transgene- free genome-edited plants were efficiently generated for various genes (either individual or multiplex) in tomato, tobacco, potato, and citrus in the T0 generation. The biallelic/homozygous transgene-free mutation rates for target genes among herbicide-resistant transformants ranged from 8% to 50%. Whole genome sequencing further confirmed transgene-free and absence of off- target mutations in the edited plants. The co-editing strategy is efficient for generating transgene- free, genome-edited plants in the T0 generation, thus being a potent tool for plant genetic improvement.",
        "assignee": "University Of Florida Research Foundation, Incorporated",
        "inventor": "JIA, Hongge",
        "translated_title": "Efficient genome editing without transgenes in plants in generation T0 based on a co-editing strategy",
        "translated_abstract": "Transgene-free genome editing of plants in T0 generation is highly desirable but challenging, especially in perennials and vegetatively propagated plants. Here is provided a co-editing strategy for generating transgene-free, gene-edited plants via Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression of cytosine base editor (CBE)/gRNA-Cas 12a/crRNA-GFP in plants. Specifically, CBE/gRNA was used to base edit the AES gene to confer resistance to herbicide chlorsulfuron as a selection marker, which has no negative effects on plant phenotypes; Casl2a/crRNA was used for editing genes(s) of interest; GFP was used for selecting transgene-free transformants. Using this approach, transgene- free genome-edited plants were efficiently generated for various genes (either individual or multiplex) in tomato, tobacco, potato, and citrus in the T0 generation. The biallelic/homozygous transgene-free mutation rates for target genes among herbicide-resistant transformants ranged from 8% to 50%. Whole genome sequencing further confirmed transgene-free and absence of off- target mutations in the edited plants. The co-editing strategy is efficient for generating transgene- free, genome-edited plants in the T0 generation, thus being a potent tool for plant genetic improvement.",

        "translated_abstract": "The application discloses a water-soluble fertilizer containing amino acids derived from L-aspartic acid fermentation waste, along with its preparation and application, and falls within the technical field of biological fermentation waste treatment. The process utilizes L-aspartic acid fermentation waste as the raw material, which is filtered to obtain biomass filter residue and a filtered stock solution. Water is added to pyrolyze the biomass filter residue, and the supernatant is collected. This supernatant is then mixed with the filtered stock solution, and the pH is adjusted to neutral to produce an amino acid solution. Finally, the amino acid solution is freeze-dried and concentrated to yield the final product. The entire preparation process is straightforward, requiring few types of materials, has low technical demands, and is highly operable. When diluted 4000 times and applied to tomatoes, the fertilizer demonstrates a significant promotional effect on tomato growth, including enhancements in plant height, stem thickness, leaf area, and chlorophyll content. It also notably increases the vitamin C content, lycopene, and soluble sugars in tomatoes, alters the variety and concentration of root secretions, and raises the proportion of acidic substances in those secretions.",

        "translated_title": "Tomato high-temperature-resistant SNP molecular marker and application thereof",
        "translated_abstract": "This invention discloses a method and system for high-throughput single nucleotide polymorphism (SNP) labeling and its applications. The invention introduces a specific high-throughput SNP labeling designated as HTS-1 and HTS-2; wherein HTS-1 is located at the 3074 bp position of the S1 CathB2-1 gene with a polymorphic form of C/T; HTS-2 is located at the 3082 bp position of the S1 CathB2-2 gene with a polymorphic form of C/G. The provided SNP labeling can be utilized for the selection of specific high-throughput traits, which can be efficiently conducted without large-scale high-throughput validation. During the growth period, it can facilitate the precise selection of high-throughput traits, enabling the identification of new varieties with specific high-throughput characteristics, thereby enhancing the yield of new varieties. The SNP labeling detection method is simplified, cost-effective, and less influenced by human factors, which can resolve issues in the evaluation of high-throughput traits in individual plants during the growth period, reducing the impact of high-throughput breeding on the yield of new varieties and ultimately improving production efficiency.",

        "inventor": "WEI LINGLING",
        "translated_title": "Method and system for predicting tomato yield based on a normal distribution function",
        "translated_abstract": "The present invention discloses a method and system for predicting the yield of crops based on a regression distribution function. When crop growth progresses into the green period, it acquires images and visual data of the crops; based on the obtained crop images, a crop growth inspection model is constructed. Furthermore, based on the acquired crop visual data, a crop growth tracking model is established. The inspection results from the crop growth inspection model are input into the crop growth tracking model to derive the crop growth tracking calculation results. Through the established regression distribution function, a statistical distribution of the crop yield at specific time intervals is calculated, allowing for the prediction of the yield in the greenhouse. This method and system leverage the regression distribution function to enhance the accuracy of crop yield predictions, providing reliable results that can adapt to varying geographical and climatic conditions, thus demonstrating strong practicality and applicability.",

        "translated_title": "Seedling and fruit separating mechanism and tomato harvester",
        "translated_abstract": "The present invention relates to a type of fruit separation machine and collection device, specifically designed for use with conveyor belts transporting fruits. It includes a separation channel for separating fruits on the conveyor belt, a fruit separation device that operates within the separation channel, and a cleaning mechanism for the separation channel. The cleaning mechanism is configured to operate in conjunction with a rotating cleaning device, which is installed within the separation channel. The separation channel is designed to be positioned below the conveyor belt, with the cleaning mechanism located between the conveyor belt and the separation channel. The direction of rotation of the cleaning mechanism is opposite to that of the conveyor belt, allowing for the effective removal of debris and contaminants from the separation channel. This design effectively prevents debris and contaminants from entering subsequent working components, thereby reducing the likelihood of damage to sensitive components and enhancing the efficiency of fruit collection. In summary, the invention provides a simplified and robust solution that significantly improves the operational reliability and efficiency of fruit separation and collection processes.",

        "title": "Deep learning-based string tomato composite picking method",
        "abstract": "The invention provides a deep learning-based string tomato compound picking method, which comprises the steps of carrying out tomato color recognition on single tomatoes in a tomato string through a depth camera and YOLOv target detection algorithm, and calculating the maturity of the tomato string; if the maturity is greater than 90%, a whole tomato string picking step is carried out, and the tomato fruit stem picking point image coordinates and depth information are obtained through a YOLOv key point detection model, so that the tomato string picking can be realized; if the maturity is between 50% and 90%, a single tomato picking step is carried out, and the tomato picking can be realized by combining the tomato fruit center point information obtained by the key point detection model; if the maturity is less than 50%, picking is not performed. The invention can realize accurate detection and positioning of tomatoes, flexibly select picking strategies according to the maturity of the tomatoes, provide various schemes for the tomato picking robot, effectively improve the picking efficiency and accuracy, reduce the false picking of unripe tomatoes and ensure the integrity of fruits.",

The reagent includes a specific primer pair consisting of an upstream primer and a downstream primer, along with a probe. The upstream primer is derived from the nucleotide sequence represented in SEQ ID NO. 1, the downstream primer is derived from the nucleotide sequence represented in SEQ ID NO. 2, and the probe is derived from the nucleotide sequence represented in SEQ ID NO. 3. This reagent for the visual rapid detection of the novel tomato leaf curl virus RPA-LFD offers several advantages: the specific primer exhibits targeted reactivity with the virus while showing no reaction with healthy hosts or other viruses, resulting in high specificity. The detection sensitivity is 1000 times greater than that of conventional immuno-colloidal gold test strips, and the method is characterized by high sensitivity. Additionally, the procedure is straightforward, user-friendly, and suitable for field applications.",

The described heating box is equipped with a pre-heating box, and the pre-heating box is configured with a connecting pipe between it and the heating box. The connecting pipe features a surface that is equipped with a spiral guide channel, and the surface of the connecting pipe is positioned on the outer side of the spiral guide channel, which has an external pre-heating pipe. In this new type of device, after the working fluid passes through the spiral guide channel in the pre-heating box, it is directed into the heating box. Subsequently, the external pre-heating pipe and the internal pre-heating pipe's electrical resistance heating element are activated to pre-heat the working fluid flowing within the spiral guide channel, thereby facilitating the continuous heating of the working fluid and reducing the burden on the heating pipe. This significantly enhances the heating efficiency of the heating device.         "translated_title": "Dryer for Processing Tomato Planting Fertilizer and Method for Drying Planting Fertilizer",
        "translated_abstract": "The invention relates to a dryer designed for processing tomato planting fertilizer and a method for drying planting fertilizer. It comprises a machine base with a rotatable barrel installed within it. The inner wall of the barrel is equipped with multiple fixed shovels arranged in a circumferential pattern around the barrel's center. Each shovel is secured at both ends with side plates, which feature two symmetrically positioned extension plates. As the moisture content of the material varies, the stir-frying height of the material is adjusted by modifying the inclination angle of the extension plates. When the material has high humidity, the angle between the extension plates and the shovels is minimized, allowing the material to slide from the highest point. Conversely, when the material is less humid, the stir-frying height is reduced by increasing the angle between the extension plates and the shovels. This mechanism effectively reduces energy consumption and production costs while ensuring the desired drying outcome.",

By constructing UBP1b transient silencing expression vector, tomato seedlings are infected, and UBP1b silencing before treatment is found to have no obvious effect on plant phenotype; after salt treatment, UBP1b silenced plants exhibited a more pronounced salt-tolerant phenotype, leaf wilting, and yellowing to a lesser extent, and plant height, root length, fresh weight of the silenced plants were higher compared to controls.         "translated_title": "Seedling raising device for planting taste tomatoes",
        "translated_abstract": "The present invention relates to a new type of open-ended sensing and planting device, specifically designed for the cultivation of crops. This new device features a crop box with two side panels at the top that are reinforced with vertical plates, and between these two plates, there are guide plates. The guide plates are equipped with a touch-sensitive component, and the interior of the crop box is fitted with a closure plate, which also contains a gripping component. The gripping component is designed to engage with multiple fixed positions at the top of the closure plate. The invention allows for the configuration of the gripping component, enabling users to activate the gripping mechanism when necessary for the crop. Users can rotate a lever to initiate movement, which subsequently causes the active plate to shift. The active plate then drives the tooth bar's movement, followed by the rotation of two toothed wheels. The toothed wheels drive a rotating shaft, which in turn activates the gripping plate's rotation. When the gripping plate engages with the crop's stem, users no longer need to rotate the lever, allowing for the gripping of the crop without requiring multiple electric machines, thus enhancing operational efficiency.",

        "translated_abstract": "The present invention discloses a type of high-efficiency red light combined gradient pressure relief device, which integrates food processing machinery technology. This device consists of a red light external component, a gradient true space component, and a type of pressure relief sealing device. The red light external component is equipped with a red light emitting device, which has a gas pipe route for the transmission of combustible gas. The supporting structure beneath the emitting device allows for the efficient operation of the gradient pressure relief device and includes a sliding mechanism for automatic adjustment. The tail end of the emitting device is designed with a guiding rail component, which is smaller at one end adjacent to the sliding wheel and larger at the end connected to the gradient pressure relief box. The gradient true space component is equipped with two true space chambers, which have different true space densities, and the inner part of the true space density chamber is fitted with an elastic pressing mechanism. The pressure relief sealing device component includes a shell body and a rotating member, which allows for the realization of different sizes of pressure relief flow entering different true space densities through openings of varying sizes on both sides of the shell body. The gradient pressure relief device significantly enhances the efficiency of the pressure relief process while reducing the loss of pressure relief quality.",

        "abstract": "The application relates to the technical field of tomato planting, in particular to a fresh tomato composite culture medium for anti-cracking fruits and application thereof. The ceramic comprises a matrix A and a matrix B, wherein inorganic matters in the matrix A comprise ceramsite, perlite and gravel; the organic matters comprise wine grape residue; the substrate B comprises a microbial agent. Under the combined action of inorganic matters, organic matters and microbial agents, the fruit cracking rate of the fresh tomatoes can be greatly reduced to 10.3 percent, and compared with the fruit cracking rate planted in common soil, the fruit cracking rate of the fresh tomatoes is reduced by 10.7 percent, and the reduction rate is 51 percent. The composite cultivation matrix is green, environment-friendly and pollution-free, and is convenient to use.         "translated_abstract": "This invention relates to modern agricultural production, specifically focusing on the application of the SlMYB52 gene in the regulation of lateral branching in plants. The invention describes the construction of transgenic plants with the SlMYB52 gene, utilizing gene knockout and expression vector techniques to modulate the expression levels of SlMYB52, thereby influencing its regulatory mechanisms on lateral branching. The results indicate that overexpression of the SlMYB52 gene significantly increases the number and length of lateral branches, while knockout of the SlMYB52 gene leads to a reduction in both the number and length of lateral branches. In the context of modern agricultural production, the enhancement of lateral branching in crops can improve yield, enhance product quality, and address challenges related to artificial cultivation.",

This configuration is utilized to effectively grasp and secure objects within a designated gripping space. When the actuator of the flexible transmission system is activated to the first position, the gripping mechanism engages the inner side of the designated gripping space, allowing the multiple actuators to form a cohesive grasp around the object. When the actuator is activated to the second position, the gripping mechanism engages the outer side of the designated gripping space, thereby enabling the effective release of the object.         "translated_abstract": "This invention relates to a breeding method in the field of plant breeding technology, specifically involving a type of green pepper, which is resistant to the Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus (TYLCV). The method utilizes a breeding strategy based on the self-pollination of a line with resistance to TYLCV, specifically the Ty-3a resistant line, to produce F1 hybrid seeds. The BC2F2 generation is further self-pollinated to produce BC2F6 generation seeds, which ultimately yield green pepper lines that are homozygous for TYLCV resistance and have a soluble solid content exceeding 5.         "translated_abstract": "The invention presents a soilless culture device and method specifically designed for Guarana parasitic tomatoes. In this system, the substrate containers of all soilless culture units are interconnected in parallel along a single medium flow pipeline. A U-shaped flow channel structure is established between any two adjacent substrate containers, allowing the application of the pressure difference principle inherent to U-shaped pipes. This configuration enables the sequential and uniform filling of liquid growth substrates based on the pressure differential created as the substrates flow through the pipeline. The liquid growth substrates are then diffused into the plant growth containers via through holes, ensuring that the root systems of both tomatoes and cucurbits have consistent contact with the liquid growth substrates. This entire process facilitates efficient soilless cultivation of large-scale and high-volume Guarana parasitic tomatoes without the need for manual filling of each substrate container, thereby enhancing the cultivation efficiency and management of Guarana parasitic tomatoes.",

        "abstract": "The application belongs to the technical field of artificial intelligence, and relates to a cherry tomato maturity identification method and device based on quantitative nuclear magnetic images. The application acquires relaxation information of six internal structures by using sample multi-echo image data, filters noise errors among voxels caused by voxel-by-voxel processing, reserves the difference of relaxation among structures, reduces the complexity of the data and improves the precision of the structural data. The method comprises the steps of obtaining a typical six-structure template of cherry tomato fruits through a deep learning structure segmentation model obtained by collecting data in batches, carrying out image structural segmentation on scanned multi-echo image data in the fruits according to the template to obtain multi-echo data of each structure (average value of six structures of each echo), carrying out single-index and three-index inversion on the echo data of each structure based on a chaotic particle swarm inversion algorithm, and presenting the water state of each structure as a whole and three components, so that very accurate maturity judgment results can be obtained, and the method has important practical significance for guiding the technological promotion of links such as storage, fresh keeping, cold chain transportation and the like and improving the quality of fruits and vegetables.",

        "translated_abstract": "The invention presents a novel energy-saving Mechanical Vapor Recompression (MVR) device for tomato sauce production, pertaining to the technical field of environmental resource energy conservation. This invention addresses the issue of decreasing concentration temperatures after each processing step in the production of tomato sauce, which necessitates continuous temperature compensation to maintain adequate concentration levels.         "translated_title": "Application of EXA1 gene in enhancing resistance of tomato brown wrinkle fruit virus",
        "translated_abstract": "This invention relates to the field of molecular biology and plant protection technology, specifically the application of the EXA1 strain in enhancing resistance against viral pathogens such as ToBRFV, TMV, and PVX in plants. Through comparative analysis of the EXA1 strain with other strains, it was found that the EXA1 strain significantly reduces viral accumulation in plants compared to control groups, indicating its potential to enhance resistance and/or reduce virulence or lethality of viral infections. The EXA1 strain is commonly present in cultivated plants, and the use of VIGS technology combined with CRISPR/Cas9 gene editing techniques allows for the targeted elimination of EXA1, leading to a reduction in viral infection severity. This provides a promising approach for developing new plant varieties with enhanced resistance against viral pathogens, thereby contributing to improved agricultural productivity and sustainability.",

        "abstract": "The invention discloses a method for improving tomato rhizosphere soil by applying biogas slurry to a biochar-based fertilizer, and belongs to the technical field of crop planting. The preparation method comprises the following steps: applying a biochar base fertilizer when tomatoes are planted in a fixed mode, applying the biochar base fertilizer for the first time when the tomatoes are planted in the fixed mode for 15 days, applying the biochar base fertilizer for the second time when the first spike of tomatoes is enlarged, and applying the biochar base fertilizer for the third time when the second spike or the third spike of tomatoes is enlarged; and (3) using the biogas slurry water solution as irrigation water after the tomato field planting. According to the invention, through the organic combination of the biochar base fertilizer and the biogas slurry, the rhizosphere soil environment of tomatoes is obviously improved, the tomato yield and the tomato quality are obviously improved, and the research and the application of the biochar base fertilizer Shi Zhaoye provided by the invention on tomatoes are beneficial to promoting agriculture to change to a more environment-friendly and more efficient direction, so that the invention accords with the current global pursuit of green ecology and sustainable development.",

        "translated_abstract": "The present invention relates to a novel type of water immersion box suitable for the cultivation of aquatic plants. The box body includes a heating rod installed in the bottom section, and one side of the box body is equipped with a light source component. One end of the box body is fixedly connected to a timer, while the other end is fixedly connected to a drainage outlet for wastewater. The drainage outlet is used for discharging wastewater, and the heating rod is employed to heat the nutrient solution. Through a turbidity sensor and control device installed within the heating rod, when the turbidity of the solution in the box body reaches a predetermined minimum turbidity (15 NTU), the heating rod will automatically check and proceed with heating. Conversely, when the turbidity reaches a predetermined maximum turbidity (25 NTU), the heating rod will automatically stop heating. The installation of the light source is designed to provide illumination for a specific duration, with a bright phase of one hour followed by a dark phase of six hours. The timing of the illumination is controlled by the timer. The cultivation system is designed for the growth of aquatic plants, ensuring optimal conditions for the cultivation process and achieving effective results in the cultivation of aquatic plants.",

        "translated_abstract": "The present invention relates to a new type of cleaning and collection device for liquid and particulate matter, including a housing body with a fixed upper connection for an electric machine. This new device utilizes a start-up push-out pipe with an automatic valve, allowing water and particulate matter to be drawn into the collection chamber through the internal push-out pipe. When water is filtered through the filter network, it can be directed into the bottom of the collection chamber, while particulate matter, upon contact with the filter network, is filtered at the upper part of the filter. When the collection chamber reaches the upper part of the filter with particulate matter, it activates a water discharge electric machine. The discharge electric machine drives a belt wheel, which operates in conjunction with a rotating shaft and a push rod. The push rod facilitates the movement of the collected particulate matter through the filter network, allowing the upper part of the particulate matter to slide towards the left side of the filter network. This design ensures that all collected particulate matter is efficiently discharged from the cleaning chamber without requiring manual intervention, thereby enhancing the cleaning effectiveness of the particulate matter collection device.",

        "translated_abstract": "The invention presents a method for grading tomato maturity based on the fusion of visual and tactile information. This method captures color characteristic data from the surface of a tomato by obtaining image information and utilizing two color space conversion techniques. Additionally, it employs the maximum inter-class variance method for image segmentation, effectively reducing the computational load of image processing. A gamma conversion algorithm is applied to enhance image differentiation, allowing for the extraction of surface glossiness characteristic information of the tomatoes. Furthermore, surface roughness characteristic information is obtained using a touch sensor in conjunction with a PID control method. This surface roughness information is then fused with the image characteristic data and input into a BP neural network for training, resulting in a tomato maturity grading model that establishes a correlation between the tomato characteristic information and its maturity grade. The method outputs a grading result for tomatoes that meet maturity standards.         "translated_abstract": "A method for lighting and cultivating tomato plants in environments without access to natural light and in greenhouse conditions is described, wherein the cultivation space is equipped with a ventilation system and environmental sensors. Humidity and temperature in the cultivation area are established, and during the growth phase, the plants are illuminated with a lighting fixture equipped with LEDs for at least 16 hours per day, ensuring that at least one tomato plant at each developmental stage (including seed) is illuminated, maintaining a light intensity of no less than 160 µmol/m²/s for 16 hours and a spectrum tailored to the plant's growth phase, such that the ratio of red to blue light (R:B) is at least 1.70 ± 0.15. During the vegetative phase, the cultivation is managed so that in both indoor and greenhouse settings, the light intensity on the upper leaf surfaces of the plant is maintained at 160 µmol/m²/s for 16 hours daily. In the vegetative phase within the cultivation tent, a spectrum consisting of white light with peaks of artificial blue and red light is maintained for 15 to 17 hours, preferably 16 hours per day, with a red to blue light ratio of 2.4 ± 15%. In the generative growth phase, the cultivation is managed to maintain a light intensity of 200 µmol/m²/s on the upper leaf surfaces for 15 to 17 hours per day, preferably for 16 hours daily.         "abstract": "The invention relates to the field of molecular genetic breeding, in particular to a specific primer combination of SNP and KASP molecular markers coseparated with tomato male sterile mutation site ms30 and application thereof. The invention discloses a tomato B type MADS-box gene Solyc G059970 which is a ms30 candidate gene, wherein a SNP exists at the 176 th bp, the polymorphism is G/T, the tomato with the base G is fertile, and the tomato with the base T is male sterile. The invention also discloses a KASP molecular marker MS30K primer combination for detecting the SNP. The molecular marker MS30K can be used for rapidly and accurately distinguishing tomato male sterile mutants, heterozygous plants and homozygous fertility normal plants containing mutation sites MS 30. Gene editing was performed on Solyc g059970, and the phenotype of the obtained mutant was similar to MS30, and was a tomato male sterile mutant, indicating that Solyc g059970 gene editing mutant and MS30 were allelic mutations, and Solyc g059970 was determined to be the MS30 gene.         "translated_title": "Automatic Control Device for Liquid Manure in Tomato Planting",
        "translated_abstract": "The present invention relates to a new type of liquid and water cultivation technology, specifically an automatic control device for liquid and a certain type of plant water cultivation. The device includes a reservoir, where the reservoir is equipped with a liquid storage tank that contains water cultivation. Through a water pump, the water cultivation from the liquid storage tank is introduced into two sets of soft pipes via three connecting pipes, and the water cultivation is discharged through two high-pressure nozzles. The system is driven by a motor that activates a main drive gear wheel, allowing the drive gear wheel to rotate in synchronization with a rotating shaft, enabling the two sets of nozzles to move. This facilitates the water cultivation discharge from different positions of the plant. A liquid level sensor monitors the water cultivation level within the liquid storage tank, and when the water cultivation level drops below a preset value, the control device shuts off the water pump while simultaneously reversing the motor to return the two sets of nozzles, allowing the operator to easily measure the water cultivation volume within the discharge tank and subsequently refill the liquid storage tank with water cultivation as needed.",

        "translated_abstract": "The invention presents an application of the SlARF gene in the regulation of plant root development and lodging resistance. The nucleotide sequence of the SlARF gene is designated as SEQ ID NO.1, while the corresponding amino acid sequence is designated as SEQ ID NO.2. Research findings indicate that inhibiting or downregulating the expression of the tomato SlARF gene enhances the development of lateral roots, suppresses the growth of the main root, and reduces stem thickening, thereby improving the lodging resistance of transgenic plants. Conversely, upregulating the expression of the SlARF gene can inhibit the growth of both the main and lateral roots and decrease stem thickness, which may lead to reduced lodging resistance in transgenic plants. The SlARF gene functions as a negative regulatory factor for plant lodging resistance and can be utilized to manipulate the growth conditions of plant roots and stems. This provides a novel molecular target for the breeding enhancement of tomatoes and other plants, thereby improving the biological functions associated with the SlARF gene.         "translated_abstract": "The invention pertains to the technical field of tomato seed byproduct production and discloses a method for extracting tomato seed byproducts. This method addresses the issues of waste and environmental pollution associated with the disposal of large quantities of tomato sauce processing waste prevalent in prior art. The specific technical process involves: (1) drying tomato seeds in a microwave oven; (2) crushing the dried tomato seeds using a crusher; (3) adding an entrainer to the crushed tomato seeds and extracting with supercritical CO2 to obtain tomato seed oil and oil meal; and (4) extracting and acid-precipitating the oil meal with alkali to yield tomato seed protein. This process offers advantages such as a high extraction rate, high-quality tomato seed oil, and a high protein content in the tomato seed protein. It reduces resource waste from tomato sauce production waste and mitigates environmental pollution, while providing health-beneficial tomato seed oil and protein-rich tomato seed protein for cereal foods.         "assignee": "æ²³åç§æå¤§å­¦",
        "inventor": "Zhao kaixuan",
        "translated_title": "String-type tomato picking device and method for determining angles of joints of multi-joint mechanical arm",
        "translated_abstract": "The present invention discloses a type of linkage mechanism and a multi-axis machine tool that can determine the angular position of each axis based on the linkage mechanism's configuration. The linkage mechanism comprises multiple axes of the machine tool and an end effector that operates under the control of a control model. The control model receives information regarding the target position of the linkage mechanism and calculates the angular positions of each axis based on the motion dynamics. It computes the required angular positions and adjusts the corresponding servo motors to the specified angles. During the motion dynamics calculation, it determines the length of the projection of the machine tool in the X and Y axes, denoted as len, and the height of the projection in the Z axis, denoted as high. By utilizing the projection lengths len and high, along with the angular values of the end effector, the system can accurately determine the angular positions of each axis of the machine tool. This invention resolves the issue of calculating the final control angle of the last axis based on the previously calculated control angles of each axis, thereby addressing the complexity of control timing issues.",

        "translated_abstract": "The present invention discloses a method for detecting and locating points of interest using a series of scanning and sensing devices. Utilizing a series of scans as the primary object, it employs advanced data processing techniques to build a dataset of scans, integrating the YOLOv5 deep learning algorithm for object detection accuracy. By applying a European-style clustering method to obtain the object clusters of interest, it extracts central coordinates of these clusters and utilizes the RANSAC line fitting algorithm to perform line fitting on the segments of the detected objects, thereby outputting the location information of the detected points of interest. The scanning and sensing devices are capable of identifying the series of scans, measuring object dimensions, color characteristics, and unmeasured object dimensions, through the generation of depth images that visualize the object clusters. The point cloud data is then processed through a European-style clustering method to extract the object clusters and perform line fitting, in order to obtain the segments of the fitted lines, with points in these segments serving as the detected points of interest for the scanning and sensing devices. The vibration generator is utilized to induce vibrations in the seedling separator, and the roller driving device is employed to facilitate the rotation of the seedling separator.
